title: Senior Counsellor
description:
Eagles Mediation & Counselling Centre (EMCC) established in 1997, is one of the region’s foremost organisations that pioneered family mediation and training, and one of the few agencies with expertise to provide integrative services in mediation and psychotherapy. 

EMCC has continued to evolve yet always endeavouring to be true to our vision of Bringing Hope and Restoration – to lost and hurting individuals, and broken relationships through our counselling and mediation services.  On the upstream, EMCC actively develops prevention and early detection of psycho-emotional and relational problems. This includes training, public talks and workshops, in-company training, community outreach programmes, resource development, among others. These are aimed at empowering individuals through imparting and equipping of knowledge and self-help skills to effectively manage their interpersonal relationship challenges.  We advocate for destigmatisation of mental health and normalise seeking help through public campaigning.

EMCC is a non-profit with Institute of Public Character status.


Job Description

We are seeking an experienced counsellor to provide counselling services, consultations, assessments and interventions for individuals, couples and families experiencing socio-emotional and mental health challenges of moderate to high complexity and risk.

The incumbent will provide clinical supervision of lesser experienced counsellors, develop, deliver and oversee the training curriculum, conduct training for other professionals and corporate clients, and lead community outreach initiatives.  As the Senior Counsellor, you must have knowledge of case management and the ability to engage and network with external stakeholders to promote the organisational objectives of EMCC.  

The successful candidate should demonstrate high professionalism, leadership, and management skills.


Responsibilities

  • Provide early-risk marriage support e.g. minor and trans-national couples, separation support and family and marital counselling.

  • Lead group counselling and other therapeutic interventions to individuals and families facing challenges; and to work with relevant partners to promote the welfare of the clients.

  • Proficient in various counselling approaches (e.g. systemic etc) and tools to help with clients' concerns effectively.

  • Possess the ability to manage challenging cases, including crisis interventions and referrals to other agencies.

  • Deliver clinical supervision for staffs’ professional development.

  • Provide oversight of high-risk cases and consultation to less experienced counsellors handling them.

  • Supervise and manage counselling interns, when necessary.

  • Identify the training needs of stakeholders and work with partners to plan, develop and implement training/programmes for the different client groups.

  • Identify gaps in clients service delivery and improve work processes.

  • Comply with workflow processes and improve protocols where required.

  • Support the Lead Counsellor in the development and implementation of the counselling and training services plan to meet organisational KPIs.

  • Ensure funded projects comply with the funding terms and conditions.

  • Ensure budgets are adhered to and financial systems maintained.

  • Provide timely reports on statutory, grants/funders for internal purposes and requirements.

  • Perform any other duties assigned by the Executive Director.

Educational Qualifications

  • Master’s Degree in Counselling or Counselling Psychology by accredited institutions under the Singapore Association for Counselling (SAC).

  • SAC registered counsellor and preferably SAC registered Clinical Supervisor.

  • Training in different modalities.


Experiences and Competencies

  • More than five years of experience in counselling individuals, couples, and families with at least two years of supervisory experience.

  • Experience in counselling all age groups (children and youths, adults, and seniors).

  • Experience in facilitating psycho-education and therapy groups.

  • Proven track record of helping clients to achieve their psychological well-being goals.

  • Experience working with different nationalities and cultures.

  • Passionate about advocating the importance of mental health.

  • A people-oriented team player that contributes to a positive work and team culture.

  • Ability to adapt and be flexible with changes.

  • Excellent written (e.g. report writing) and presentation skills.

  • Committed, able to multi-task and possess a can-do attitude.

postedCompany.description:
About EMCC

Since our establishment on Tue, Oct 21, 1997, we have endeavoured to bring hope to broken relationships. We believe the Singaporean society at large benefits from the development of strong marriages and healthy families.


We are Eagles Mediation & Counselling Centre Ltd (UEN: 201904577Z) – EMCC. We are a registered charity, a full member of the National Council of Social Service (NCSS), and an approved Institution of Public Character (IPC).


For couples and families who want to repair or strengthen their relationships, EMCC uses evidence-based methodologies to help them manage conflicts healthily and improve their relationships. This is done through our range of services: mediating disputes, providing marital and family counselling, and conducting educational workshops. We are also actively developing upstream initiatives towards the prevention and early detection of psychoemotional and relational problems.


EMCC is one of the few agencies with the expertise to be a one-stop centre for relational restoration and wellness. We provide both mediation and psycho-therapy as a holistic and integrated approach to addressing relational disputes or difficulties.

VISION

Bringing Hope to Relationships

MISSION

EMCC enables people to effectively manage personal, marital, family and organisational
challenges and conflicts.
postedCompany.name: EAGLES MEDIATION & COUNSELLING CENTRE LTD.
